revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大正天皇祭]]
'''Taisho Emperor Festival''' (Taisho Tennosai) was the name of the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} during the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa period|1=Shōwa era}}. Following the passing of {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taisho|1=Emperor Taishō}} on {{ill|December 25, 1926|ja|1926年12月25日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 25, 1926|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was conducted annually on the anniversary of his passing as a grand festival (where the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} led the ceremony with {{ill|Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} and {{ill|Bureaucrat|en|Bureaucrat|qid=Q572700|lt=bureaucrats|1=Bureaucrat}}) at the {{ill|Koreiden|ja|皇霊殿|lt=Kōrei-den (Imperial Ancestral Shrines)|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koreiden|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮中三殿}}.
Since {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}}, the Previous Emperor Festival has been changed to the {{ill|Showa Emperor Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa Emperor Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (held on {{ill|January 7|en|January 7|qid=Q2228|lt=January 7|1=January 7}}). The rites for Emperor Taisho, like those for {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|qid=Q299179|lt=Emperor Komei|1=Emperor Kōmei}} and {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}, are now observed as the '''Taisho Emperor Annual Festival''' (Taisho Tennou Reisa), which is conducted annually on the anniversary of his passing as a minor festival (where the emperor leads a prayer while the {{ill|Chief Ritualist|ja|掌典長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Ritualist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} conducts the ceremony). On designated anniversaries (3rd, 5th, 10th, 20th, 30th, 40th, 50th, 100th years and every 100 years thereafter), the '''Taisho Emperor Ceremonial Festival''' (Taisho Tennou Shikinen-sai) is held as a grand festival.
== Overview == On the day of the ceremony, an {{ill|Imperial envoy|ja|勅使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial envoy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Chokushi|qid=Q11399920}} is dispatched to {{ill|Tama Mausoleum|ja|武蔵陵墓地#大正天皇陵「多摩陵」|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Mausoleum|1=Musashi Imperial Graveyard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6940153}}, where the {{ill|Heihō-no-Gi|ja|奉幣の儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heihō-no-Gi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is performed.
The {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, {{ill|Kanpei-sha shrines|ja|官幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha shrines|1=kokuheisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452104}}, and other general shrines also conduct rituals, with a focus on remote worship of the mausoleum. The ceremonies at the Imperial Court include three parts: the {{ill|Koreiden Ceremony|ja|皇霊殿の儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koreiden Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} honoring the spirit in the {{ill|Koreiden|ja|皇霊殿|lt=Kōrei-den (Imperial Ancestral Shrines)|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koreiden|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮中三殿}}, the {{ill|Mikagura Ceremony|ja|御神楽の儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagura Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the {{ill|Heihō-no-Gi|ja|奉幣の儀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heihō-no-Gi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mausoleum.
These are observed annually as part of the regular rites, but in designated anniversary years, a more solemn {{ill|Shikinen Festival|ja|式年祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Festival|1=Shikinen-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487032}} is conducted. During these occasions, the emperor personally visits the mausoleum for the {{ill|Goyuko Gosai|ja|行幸御親祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goyuko Gosai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the {{ill|Chief Ritualist|ja|掌典長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Ritualist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} performs the rites at the Koreiden in his place.
The origins of the Previous Emperor Festival trace back to the {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuki|1=Q11420927|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420927}}, as stipulated in the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taiho Code|1=Taihō Code}}, where it was stated that "On a national mourning day, the sovereign ceases official duties," and certain temples were designated for officials to perform purification rites.
Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the {{ill|Jimmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu Emperor Festival|1=Emperor Jimmu Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590440}} and Previous Emperor Festival were officially designated as state holidays. On {{ill|October 14, 1873|ja|明治6年10月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 14, 1873|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Annual Holidays and Festival Days Act|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Holidays and Festival Days Act|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6 Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was enacted, setting specific festival and holiday dates. The {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (January 30) was included as a Previous Emperor Festival alongside the Jimmu Emperor Festival (April 3). On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|昭和2nd year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Holidays Act|ja|休日ニ関スル件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holidays Act|1=Regarding Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381150}} (Showa 2 Imperial Ordinance No. 25) further defined public holidays, adding the {{ill|Emperor Taisho Festival|ja|大正天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Taisho Festival|1=Emperor Taishō Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11436695}} (December 25) to the list, alongside the previously designated {{ill|Emperor Meiji Festival|ja|明治天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Meiji Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (July 30).
It is believed that between 1927 and 1947, when December 25 was designated as a national holiday, the widespread adoption of {{ill|Christmas|en|Christmas|qid=Q19809|lt=Christmas|1=Christmas}} in Japan was significantly influenced by the holiday status of the date.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|大正天皇祭|version=94756264|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:大正天皇祭]]
<!--Taisho Festival--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11436695}} [[Category:December]] [[Category:Emperor Taishō]] [[Category:Japanese Imperial Rituals]] [[Category:Pre-war Japanese public holidays]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
